title: Grainy Algerian Lentils Pie
description: A resinous, snappy one-pot Algerian dish featuring puff pastry, sautéed spices, and hearty lentils, finished in a bamboo tray.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C and line a bamboo tray with parchment paper.
Rinse the lentils until the water runs clear.
In a saucepan, heat olive oil over medium heat and sauté the diced onion and minced garlic until translucent.
Add cumin, coriander, paprika, and oregano, cooking for 1 minute to release aromas.
Stir in the lentils, tomato paste, and vegetable broth, then bring the mixture to a simmer, cover, and cook for 15 minutes or until lentils are tender.
Season the lentil bake with salt and pepper, remove from heat, and let it cool slightly.
Unroll the puff pastry sheet and place it on the prepared bamboo tray, trimming edges if needed.
Spoon the cooled lentil mixture onto the pastry, spreading it evenly across the surface.
Fold any excess pastry over the edges to seal the filling, and cut a small slit in the top for steam.
Bake for 25 minutes until the pastry is golden brown and crisp.
